title: Encapsulation & Special Methods
description: Data hiding and Python's magic methods.

slides.title: Hiding Implementation 🔒
slides.content:
# Encapsulation

**Encapsulation** hides internal details:

```python
class BankAccount:
    def __init__(self):
        self._balance = 0  # "Private" attribute
```

`_underscore` indicates "don't touch directly!"

slides.title: Naming Conventions 📝
slides.content:
# Python's Privacy Convention

| Prefix | Meaning |
|--------|--------|
| `name` | Public — use freely |
| `_name` | Protected — internal use |
| `__name` | Private — name mangled |

Python uses conventions, not strict rules!

slides.title: Protected Attributes 💻
Special Methods ✨
slides.content:
# Magic/Dunder Methods

Methods like `__init__` are **special methods**:

| Method | Called When |
|--------|-------------|
| `__init__` | Object created |
| `__str__` | str(obj) or print(obj) |
| `__len__` | len(obj) |
| `__eq__` | obj1 == obj2 |

slides.title: __str__ Method 💻

quizData.question: What does __str__ define?
quizData.explanation: __str__ defines what print() and str() return for the object!

slides.title: Comparison Methods 💻
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Common Special Methods

* `__len__` — `len(obj)`
* `__getitem__` — `obj[key]`
* `__contains__` — `x in obj`
* `__add__` — `obj1 + obj2`

Define these to make your classes behave like built-in types!

quizData.question: What are dunder methods?
quizData.explanation: Dunder (double underscore) methods like __init__ are special methods!
